name: Code Climate Code Quality and Engineering Analytics API
what_it_does: Code Climate code quality and engineering analytics REST API for engineering leaders to measure code quality, test coverage, technical debt, and team velocity across repositories with CI/CD integration and trend reporting. Enables AI agents to manage repository code quality analysis and trend tracking for engineering health automation, handle test coverage reporting and threshold enforcement for quality gate automation, access maintainability rating and technical debt scoring for codebase health automation, retrieve code smell and issue detection for refactoring prioritization automation, manage CI/CD pipeline quality check integration for automated quality gates, handle engineering metrics and velocity reporting for team performance automation, access churn analysis and code stability tracking for risk identification automation, retrieve pull request and diff-level analysis for code review automation, manage organization-wide code health dashboard for engineering leadership automation, and integrate Code Climate with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Jira, and Slack for end-to-end engineering quality automation.
use_cases: Tracking code quality from engineering health automation agents
Enforcing coverage from CI/CD quality gate agents
Measuring technical debt from codebase health agents
Monitoring team velocity from engineering metrics agents
not_for: Security vulnerability scanning (use Snyk or Semgrep for security-focused SAST)
Application performance monitoring (use Datadog or New Relic)
Code review workflow management (use GitHub PR reviews or Reviewable)
best_when: An engineering team or engineering leader wanting AI agents to automate code quality monitoring, test coverage enforcement, technical debt tracking, and engineering velocity measurement through Code Climate's quality and analytics platform.
avoid_when: CODE CLIMATE VELOCITY vs QUALITY AS SEPARATE PRODUCTS: Code Climate offers Quality (code analysis) and Velocity (engineering metrics) as separate products with separate APIs; automated engineering analytics workflows must integrate both products for complete picture; conflating Quality and Velocity API endpoints creates data retrieval errors. TEST COVERAGE THRESHOLD ENFORCEMENT IN CI REQUIRES REPORTER SETUP: Code Climate test coverage enforcement in CI requires test reporter binary installed in CI pipeline; automated coverage gate enforcement cannot be configured via API alone; CI pipeline setup required for each repository before API-based coverage threshold automation is effective. TECHNICAL DEBT RATING METHODOLOGY CHANGE AWARENESS: Code Climate technical debt ratings (A-F maintainability score) may change when Code Climate updates rating algorithm; automated quality trend reports must document algorithm version for longitudinal comparisons; automated quality score comparisons across algorithm versions without normalization creates misleading trend analysis.

auth.notes: Code Climate uses API key and OAuth for integrations. REST API with JSON. New York, NY HQ. Founded 2011 by Bryan Helmkamp. Backed by Accomplice, CRV ($5M+ raised). Products: Code Climate Quality (code analysis, coverage), Code Climate Velocity (engineering metrics). GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket integration. GDPR. Serves engineering teams and leaders. Competes with SonarQube, Codacy, and DeepSource for code quality.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for open source repositories; private repos require paid plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Quality: $16/seat/month (up to 10 seats); custom for larger teams; Velocity: separate pricing
pricing.notes: New York NY. Accomplice/CRV backed. Free for open source. Per-seat for private repos. Quality and Velocity separate.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; analysis and metrics queries throttled; webhooks for analysis completion and coverage events

known_agent_gotchas: ANALYSIS TRIGGER vs ANALYSIS RESULT TIMING: Code Climate analysis is triggered by push to connected repository; API retrieval of analysis results must wait for analysis completion; automated quality gate that checks results immediately after push encounters in-progress analysis state; implement analysis status polling with completion webhook rather than immediate result fetch

REPOSITORY SLUG vs REPOSITORY ID IN API: Code Climate API uses both repository slug (org/repo format) and numeric ID in different endpoints; automated repository management must maintain correct identifier type per endpoint; automated multi-repository quality monitoring must handle identifier type requirement per operation

COVERAGE REPORTER BINARY VERSION FOR CI INTEGRATION: Code Climate test coverage requires specific reporter binary version in CI; binary version mismatch between CI and Code Climate backend creates coverage report parsing failure; automated CI setup must pin correct reporter version and verify compatibility with Code Climate backend version

CHURN METRIC CALCULATION WINDOW CONFIGURATION: Code Climate churn (code change frequency) is calculated over configurable time window (default 90 days); automated risk analysis using churn must document and be consistent with time window configuration; comparing churn across repositories with different window configurations creates misleading relative risk assessment

VELOCITY METRICS GITHUB INTEGRATION SCOPE: Code Climate Velocity requires GitHub organization OAuth with broad repository read permission; automated engineering metrics pipeline must authorize Velocity with organizational scope; repository-level authorization creates incomplete Velocity metrics that miss cross-repository engineering patterns
agent_readiness.security_notes: Code quality. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Code metrics and repository data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Code quality. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US. Code metrics and repository data.
